eq2 = (x + 1)**2 + (y - 2)**2 - 25

eq_diff = sp.simplify(eq1 - eq2)

items.response: •	The “C” in SMART stands for “Specific.” This means that the goal should be clear, well-defined, and achievable. A vague or vague goal is unlikely to be effective in motivating you to take action. For example, instead of setting a goal to “get in shape,” set a specific goal to “run a 5K race in under 25 minutes by June 30th.” •	The “M” in SMART stands for “Measurable.” This means that the goal should be quantifiable or have a way to track progress. A goal that is not measurable can be difficult to evaluate and adjust as needed. For example, instead of setting a goal to “improve your writing,” set a measurable goal to “write 500 words per day for 30 days.” •	The “A” in SMART stands for “Attainable” or “Achievable.” This means that the goal should be challenging but realistic. Setting a goal that is too easy may not provide the motivation to achieve it, while setting a goal that is too difficult may lead to frustration and failure. For example, if you are a beginner runner, setting a goal to run a marathon in 3 months might not be attainable, but setting a goal to complete a 5K in 3 months might be more achievable. •	The “R” in SMART stands for “Relevant.” This means that the goal should be relevant and relevant to your current situation, interests, and values. A goal that is not relevant to you may not provide the motivation to work towards it. For example, if you are passionate about photography, setting a goal to improve your photography skills and take more photos might be more relevant and motivating than setting a goal to learn a new language. •	The “T” in SMART stands for “Time-bound.” This means that the goal should have a specific deadline or time frame. A goal without a time frame can be open-ended and may lack the urgency needed to take action. For example, instead of setting a goal to “read more books,” set a time-bound goal to “read one book per month for the next 6 months.” By incorporating the SMART criteria into your goal-setting process, you can create goals that are clear, achievable, and aligned with your values and priorities. This can help you stay motivated and focused on making progress towards your desired outcomes.
